working: Alumni eNews - July 2026 (sent Thu 7/9) is the best 2026 send: 56.2% open, +77.2% traffic lift (14.5 to 25.7 daily sessions), and the first send ever to register tagged email sessions (email_sessions_3d = 4).
Open rates remain roughly double the 25-35% health benchmark: account average 52.1% across 100 campaigns, with the July Alumni send at 56.2%.
Reminder/follow-up sends keep beating originals: Alumni Final Reminder (6/3) +65.2% and Staff-Client eNews May (5/19) +66.5%, vs the original Alumni eNews June (5/27) at -9.8%.
Channel mix is stable: Organic Search holds 33.6% (10,732 sessions) behind Direct 50.8% (16,253) over the 1,100-day window.
not_working: UTM leakage is still near-total: 2026 sends show 4 email_sessions_3d vs 1,173 direct_sessions_3d, and GA4 credits Email only 9 sessions in the whole 1,100-day window - newsletter clicks land in Direct.
Click rates stay under benchmark despite great opens: the last four sends clicked 0.69-1.18% (July Alumni 0.78%, CTOR 1.38%) vs the 2.03% account average - readers open but do not click through.
Staff-Client eNews - June 2026 (7/2, 8,894 sends - the largest list) read -25.3% lift with 0 tagged email sessions; the biggest audience produced no measurable site traffic on a holiday-week send.
GSC slipped vs the 7/9 run: clicks 23 to 8, impressions 3,310 to 1,878, impression-weighted position 17.7 to 21.2, and pages with impressions 84 to 66.
Organic Social remains marginal at 5.4% of sessions (1,733) across the full window.
do_next: Finish UTM tagging (utm_source=constantcontact&utm_medium=email) on all newsletter links: the 7/9 send finally registered 4 tagged email sessions, proving the tags work when present - roll them into the Staff-Client and Referring Partners templates before the next send.
Investigate the GSC drop (clicks 23 to 8, position 17.7 to 21.2 vs 7/9): verify recently published pages are live on production and re-run the keyword opportunity finder on slipping queries.
Lift sub-1% click rates with one clear CTA block per newsletter: the May Alumni send hit 1.79% click with event-style content, and the all-time top lifts are all event/alumni themed.

Keep the Thursday + reminder cadence and avoid holiday-adjacent double-sends: 7/9 (+77.2%) and the reminder sends (+65%) beat the 7/2 holiday double-send (-25.3%); schedule a reminder ~1 week after each Alumni send.
summary: The July 9 Alumni eNews was the strongest 2026 send (56.2% open, +77.2% lift) and the first ever with tagged email sessions, but UTM leakage still hides nearly all email traffic inside Direct (4 vs 1,173 post-send sessions in 2026), and GSC clicks/position slipped versus the last run.
